_README_BOOST_CHAIN: Why some 'remote' vanilla DPs still ship the boosted variant even when include_pickups=false:

MT validates its mission/cargo registry at world load. Shipping a boosted cargo (e.g. Fuelx2) that NO vanilla DP references crashes the game — empirically reproduced. The framework keeps a minimum vanilla mission-graph footprint by injecting the boosted variant into a SAFETY-NET subset of vanilla DPs (the consumers of the source cargo), even when include_pickups is off.

Practical implication: with include_pickups=false plus the default safety net, two vanilla DP classes (Farm_Cabbage_C and Farm_Hemp_C by default) silently accept the boosted variant alongside vanilla Fuel. Their world instances will accept Fuelx2 deliveries and pay out at the boosted rate — that's the chain cost of having Fuelx2 exist at all in this MT build.

Tunable via the top-level boost_safety_dps list: pick less-played destinations or DPs that already have a stricter recipe so the boost leakage is minimized. Setting boost_safety_dps to [] is NOT supported — you'll crash on world load.

If/when MT's load-time validation gets clearer, this constraint may be replaced by a single off-map placeholder DP. Until then, treat the safety-net leakage as the price of admission for any boosted cargo.

cargo_payment_overrides._: Per-cargo PaymentPer1Km + BasePayment in-place multiplier. Mutates the existing cargo row — the cargo NAME stays the same, so vanilla missions also get the boost. Use sparingly; this is a global price knob, not a per-DP boost.

cargo_base_overrides._: Sets BasePayment to an ABSOLUTE value on the named cargo row (post-multiplier when boost also applies). Targets vanilla rows (e.g. 'Fuel': 5000) or boosted variants (e.g. 'Fuelx2': 100000). Critical for offroad routes — PaymentPer1Km only matters when MT's road-distance calc finds a marked road; offroad routes earn almost entirely from BasePayment.

cargo_spawn_overrides._: Sets SpawnProbability on the named cargo row. 0 hides the cargo from MT's random/ambient mission generator (in theory — empirical effect on boosted variants was inconclusive). Targets vanilla rows or boosted variants. No auto-default; only takes effect when explicitly listed.

cargo_sqrt_overrides._: Sets PaymentSqrtRatio on the named cargo row (default 1.0). MT scales delivery payment by a sqrt-based curve; values < 1 flatten the curve (lower payouts on long routes), > 1 amplify it. Useful for taming runaway boosted-cargo payouts without touching BasePayment / PaymentPer1Km.

_doc.purpose: Per-name delivery-point configuration. Scene placeholders named DeliveryPoint_<KEY> resolve to the entry under <KEY> here. Missing entries are logged and ignored.
_doc.naming: Scene placeholder asset_key 'DeliveryPoint_My_Cool_Spot' looks up KEY 'My_Cool_Spot'. The in-game label defaults to KEY with underscores replaced by spaces ('My Cool Spot'), capped at 14 chars (source label byte budget).
_doc.auto_derived: target_class + target_path are always auto-generated. source_class + source_actor default from the chosen `template` but can be overridden per entry for experimental clones.
_doc.validation: Cargo names + cargo types are cross-checked against CargoImport/cargos/. Invalid names produce a warning and are dropped from that recipe (don't crash the build).

fields.label: Optional in-game name. Default: KEY with '_' -> ' '. Max 14 chars (source label byte budget — varies by template).
fields.template: Optional. Selects the vanilla DP class to clone. Default: 'farm' (Farm_Corn_C/CornFarm_2 — verified end-to-end). Other classes (Container_ExportImport_C, ComonDrop_C, Factory_*) have shown crashes / persistence issues; add new templates to bp_registry._TEMPLATES once validated.
fields.source_class: Optional override. Bypasses `template` to clone a specific BP class. Experimental — may not work depending on the class's transitive dependencies.
fields.source_actor: Optional override. Pairs with source_class. Must name a vanilla instance of source_class that exists in Jeju_World.umap.
fields.recipes: List of production configs. See _example below for every supported field.
fields.marker_color: RESERVED. Hex color for the world-map marker. Pipeline propagates the value but MTBPInjector mutation is not yet wired (see AGENTS.md 'Marker / Icon Mutation (Pending)').
fields.icon: RESERVED. /Game/... path for the marker icon. Same plumbing status as marker_color.
fields.output_storage_cap: RESERVED. Per-cargo max storage on this delivery point (vanilla default ~100). Lives on a separate MTDeliveryPointInventoryRatio actor, not on the BP CDO — needs targeted mutation. Pipeline propagates the value but MTBPInjector mutation pending.
fields.include_pickups: TOP-LEVEL bool (default true). When true, EVERY vanilla DP that consumes a source cargo also accepts the boosted variant (input-only wholesale injection — the boost leaks into the entire mission graph). When false, ONLY a hand-picked SAFETY-NET subset is touched (see boost_safety_dps). The safety net is non-negotiable — leaving zero vanilla consumers crashes MT at world load because a boosted cargo with no in-world destination breaks the mission/cargo registry validation. See top-level _README_BOOST_CHAIN.
fields.boost_safety_dps: TOP-LEVEL list of vanilla DP class names that ALWAYS get the boosted variant added to inputs, regardless of include_pickups. These DPs will accept and pay out for the boosted variant in-game — pick destinations whose payout leakage is acceptable. Defaults to ['Farm_Cabbage_C', 'Farm_Hemp_C'] when the key is omitted (low-traffic farms). Empty list NOT supported — game will crash on world load.

recipe_fields.inputs: {Cargo: Count} — specific cargo names from cargos/cargo_names.txt.
recipe_fields.input_types: [Type] OR {Type: Count} — EDeliveryCargoType filter from cargos/types.txt. Accepts ANY cargo of that type.
recipe_fields.output_types: [Type] OR {Type: Count} — same shape as input_types.
recipe_fields.time_seconds: Float — ProductionTimeSeconds for one production cycle.
recipe_fields.boosted: Magic key inside an inputs/outputs cargo dict. Value is the per-km payment multiplier. For every NAMED cargo on the same side, the framework clones a boosted variant in Cargos_01.uasset (named '<Source>x<N>', e.g. Fuelx2) with PaymentPer1Km and BasePayment ×N, and adds it to THIS recipe alongside the source at the same count. Per-recipe — only DPs that opt in produce/accept the boosted variant. SIDE EFFECT (chain cost): the boost ALSO leaks into vanilla DPs via the safety-net injection — see top-level _README_BOOST_CHAIN. Set include_pickups=false + boost_safety_dps to a small list to minimize leakage; leakage cannot be reduced to zero. Combine with cargo_base_overrides[<boosted_name>] to hard-set BasePayment for offroad-friendly payouts.
recipe_fields.direct_boosted: Alternative to the `boosted` key: name the boosted cargo directly, e.g. `outputs: {"Fuelx2": 1}`. Pattern `<vanilla_cargo>x<int>` or `<cargo>x<int>p<frac>` (Fuelx2p5 = 2.5x) auto-creates the row at the implied multiplier. Use this when you want ONLY the boosted variant on a side (e.g. TF2 outputs Fuelx2 only, with no vanilla Fuel). Display label falls back to the source cargo's localized name — Fuelx2 currently shows as 'Fuel' in the mission UI; a separate-path StringTable for distinct labels is pending.

recipes._: TF2 is the consumer side of the A/B test. Accepts BOTH vanilla Fuel and boosted Fuelx2 — both produce CornBox. Players can deliver either cargo here and compare the payouts (Fuel = vanilla 300/km + vanilla base; Fuelx2 = 600/km + 100k base from cargo_base_overrides). With include_pickups=false, this is the ONLY in-world destination for Fuelx2.

TF1 spawns Fuelx2 out of thin air on a timer (no inputs needed). With include_pickups=false, this is the only in-world source of Fuelx2. Players pick up here and deliver to TF2 for the boosted payout.
Multi-cargo input + multi-cargo output, custom speed + time.
Multi-cargo input + multi-cargo output, custom speed + time.
Timer-only background production — no inputs, just outputs over time.
Cargo-TYPE filter (any LargePackage accepted as input). List form.
Cargo-type filter as map with counts.
Type-to-type recipe — accept any Sand-type cargo, produce any Concrete-type.
